name: config
context: Initialize and configure ESLint flat config for JavaScript/TypeScript projects with rules, plugins, and ignores
info: This skill covers creating and maintaining ESLint configuration files using the flat config format (eslint.config.js/mjs/cjs). It handles initializing ESLint in new projects, configuring language options (ecmaVersion, sourceType, globals), setting up ignores, integrating shareable configs like @eslint/js recommended, and structuring config arrays for multi-environment projects. Agents use this skill when setting up ESLint from scratch, adding ESLint to an existing project, or restructuring configuration. See [[eslint/rules]] for rule-level configuration, [[eslint/plugins]] for plugin integration, and [[eslint/flat-config]] for the underlying format reference.
triggers: set up eslint in my project

sections.title: Initialization
sections.body: Run `npm init @eslint/config@latest` for guided setup that detects your framework and creates `eslint.config.mjs`. For manual setup, install `eslint` and `@eslint/js`, then create the config file. The guided setup handles TypeScript, React, Vue, and other framework detection automatically.

sections.title: Language options and globals
sections.body: Set `languageOptions.ecmaVersion` for JS version, `sourceType` for module system, and `globals` for global variables. Install the `globals` package for predefined sets: `globals.browser`, `globals.node`, `globals.jest`, `globals.mocha`. For TypeScript, the parser is set automatically by typescript-eslint. See [[eslint/plugins]] for parser plugins.

sections.title: Ignore patterns
sections.body: Global ignores must be a standalone config object with ONLY the `ignores` key — no `files`, `rules`, or other properties. Patterns use glob syntax. Common ignores: `dist/`, `build/`, `node_modules/`, `coverage/`, `**/*.min.js`. Per-config ignores can coexist with `files` to exclude subsets. Note: dotfiles are NOT ignored by default in flat config — add `**/.*` if needed.

sections.title: TypeScript setup
sections.body: Install `typescript-eslint` and use `tseslint.config()` to merge configs. Spread `tseslint.configs.recommended` for recommended TS rules. For type-aware linting, set `parserOptions.projectService: true` and `tsconfigRootDir: import.meta.dirname`. See [[eslint/plugins]] for typescript-eslint details.

sections.title: React setup
sections.body: Install `eslint-plugin-react` and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`. Set `ecmaFeatures: { jsx: true }` in parserOptions. Spread `reactHooks.configs.recommended.rules` for hooks rules. Disable `react/react-in-jsx-scope` for React 17+ (automatic JSX runtime). Set `settings.react.version` to `"detect"` for auto-detection.

links.context: Add plugins for TypeScript, React, etc.

related.note: Flat config is the underlying format this skill configures
Plugins are configured within the config file
Rules are a key part of configuration

scripts.name: Initialize ESLint Config
scripts.description: Detect project type and create an appropriate eslint.config.mjs with recommended rules, plugins, and ignores

author: dojo-community
